errorMessage: Task Stopped Due to Insufficient Credits
plainLanguageMeaning: Your account ran out of monthly credits, so Lindy paused your agents and put new tasks on hold until you have credits again.

causes.title: Monthly credit allowance exhausted
causes.explanation: Credits don't roll over between billing cycles; once they're spent, agents pause until the cycle resets or you upgrade.

causes.title: An agent looping and burning credits fast
causes.explanation: When an agent gets stuck on something it can't solve, it can loop and consume credits rapidly, draining the balance unexpectedly.

causes.title: High-volume triggers driving many runs
causes.explanation: Triggers that fire frequently multiply credit usage and can exhaust the balance faster than expected.

fixSteps.instruction: Once you have credits again, manually retry the stopped task from the Tasks View.
Go to Settings > Billing to upgrade your plan if you frequently hit the limit.
Reduce consumption: filter high-volume triggers, switch steps to cheaper models, and add exit conditions so agents don't loop.
Watch for the low-credit email notification Lindy sends so you can act before agents pause.

whyItHappens: Lindy meters usage in credits that reset each billing cycle and do not carry over. Every model call and action spends credits, so a busy or looping agent can deplete the balance; when it hits zero, Lindy pauses execution rather than running up an unbounded bill, and queues new tasks until credits are restored.
verificationNote: The official credits doc describes this behavior but does not quote a verbatim string — confirm the exact UI wording and where it appears (Task History vs. email), and current plan credit numbers.
